title: Buenos Aires → Lincoln same-day road trip

route.summary: CABA → Acceso Oeste / RN7 → Luján bypass → San Andrés de Giles → Carmen de Areco → Chacabuco → Junín → RN188 → Lincoln. Return the same way.

scheduleHint.plan: Quick stop Carmen de Areco / Chacabuco (fuel, coffee, bathroom)
Plaza Rivadavia → Museo Histórico / Parque San Martín
Leave Lincoln; one short stop on the return if needed

places.name: Basílica de Luján
places.note: Spectacular, but off the Luján bypass. Only if you accept a real detour early outbound or late return — not both.

places.name: Puente Viejo
places.note: Old bridge over the Areco river — classic photo stop a short walk from the plaza. No hours to worry about.

places.name: Parque Natural Laguna de Gómez
places.address: Cam. al Parque Natural Laguna de Gómez, Junín
places.sundayHours: Park access generally open; facilities vary by season
places.note: Nice lagoon park SW of Junín — a real detour off the RN7→RN188 through-line. Only if the day is running early.

places.name: ACA Carmen de Areco YPF
places.note: Best on-route fuel / bathroom / coffee stop roughly halfway. Zero sightseeing detour — right on RN7.

places.name: Museo Gauchesco Ricardo Güiraldes
places.address: Cam. Ricardo Güiraldes s/n, San Antonio de Areco
places.note: Signature gaucho museum (Don Segundo Sombra). Sunday-open with guided visits 11:30 & 15:00. Same Parque Criollo complex as Pulpería La Blanqueada. SAA detour + museum usually conflicts with a sane Lincoln lunch — pick one focus.

places.name: Plaza Ruiz de Arellano
places.note: Not Carmen de Areco — this is the famous gaucho town ~20–35 km north of RN7 (via Giles or Carmen). Compact historic center; worth it only if you trade Lincoln time or leave BA earlier.

places.name: Parque Municipal Intendente Borchex
places.note: Big central park near downtown Junín — better short stretch than driving out to Laguna de Gómez on a same-day clock.

places.name: Plaza 25 de Mayo
places.note: Last proper town before the RN188 turn to Lincoln. Good stretch + café stop; church is on the plaza.

places.name: Plaza San Martín
places.note: Useful later coffee/bathroom town before Junín. Stay on the plaza — no need to hunt museums on a same-day clock.

places.name: Camping Balneario Carmen de Areco
places.note: River/green space north of the plaza. Fine stretch stop, but a few minutes off the RN7 through-line — skip if you're only fueling at ACA.

places.name: Museo Las Lilas de Areco
places.note: Molina Campos / costumbrista painting museum — one of SAA's best indoor stops and confirmed Sunday-open. Last entry ~60 min before each close.

places.name: Parque Municipal San Andrés de Giles
places.address: San Andrés de Giles (Parque Municipal Dr. Fernando Lillia area)
places.note: 15-ha green lung with picnic areas and paths. Worth it only if you want a longer Giles stretch; otherwise stick to the plaza + station.

places.name: Plaza Rivadavia
places.address: Av. Massey / Belgrano / Av. Alem / Antas García, Lincoln
places.note: Zero-detour town feel right after lunch. Main square with Rivadavia monument and shade — do this first.

places.name: Parque Municipal General San Martín
places.note: Lincoln's green lung and best photo stop: old trees, eco-museum area, weekend local life. Do this last, then leave town.

places.name: Plaza San Martín
places.address: San Martín 627, San Andrés de Giles
places.note: Earlier RN7 town stop (~1.5h from CABA). Pair with parish exterior, train station, or Parque Municipal nearby. Tourism office on the plaza Sun 10–15.

places.name: Plaza Independencia
places.note: On-route Carmen (not San Antonio de Areco). Short town-center stretch if you want more than a gas-station stop; keep it under 20–30 minutes.

places.name: El Show De Las Papas Fritas
places.note: Casual fries/snack backup, not a proper lunch. Useful if Flamenco is slammed or you want something before the drive back.

places.name: Edison Café
places.note: Solid coffee/pastry stop before the drive back — Sunday opens at 16:00, so it fits after park/museum time. Confirm on @edisoncafeok; hours can shift.

places.name: MUMA — Museo Municipal de Arte
places.note: Opens too late for outbound; only a late return stop. Prefer Parque Borchex / plaza if you're just stretching in Junín.

places.name: Pepperoni
places.note: Strong lunch/dinner alternative near Plaza Rivadavia (631 reviews). Lunch window closes 15:00 — same arrive-before-midafternoon rule as Flamenco.

places.name: Flamenco
places.sundayHours: Sunday 12:00–14:30 (Google); municipal listing also notes Tue–Sun lunch + dinner windows
places.note: Best sit-down lunch in town. Arrive before ~13:30. Safe bets: pasta with tomato/cheese, tortilla de papa, provoleta, fries — don't rely on a custom vegetable plate.

places.name: Usina Vieja — Museo de la Ciudad
places.address: Alsina y Matheu / Belgrano area, San Antonio de Areco
places.note: Town-history museum in the old power plant, central SAA. Confirmed weekend hours Sat–Sun 08:00–20:00; free entry commonly reported.

places.name: Complejo Museográfico Enrique Udaondo
places.note: Next to the basilica. Same Luján-detour tax — pick basilica OR museum, not a full Luján day.

places.name: Kulto Beer & Tapas
places.note: Popular tapas/beer spot (506 reviews) but Google Sunday opens 19:00 — after a sane leave-by-17:30. Only if you stay late or return another day.

places.name: Parroquia San Antonio de Padua
places.address: Bartolomé Mitre 366, San Antonio de Areco
places.sundayHours: Exterior always accessible; Sunday mass schedule varies
places.note: On the main plaza. Easy exterior stop if you already detoured into San Antonio de Areco.

places.name: Pulpería La Blanqueada
places.address: Cam. Ricardo Güiraldes, San Antonio de Areco
places.note: Restored pulpería inside the Parque Criollo / Güiraldes complex (scene from Don Segundo Sombra). Same ticket/hours as the museum — don't schedule separately.

places.name: Museo Histórico Municipal
places.address: Inside Parque Municipal Gral. San Martín, Lincoln
places.note: Best indoor stop after lunch. Inside the park — combine with a park walk. Afternoon hours fit a ~14:00–16:00 visit.

places.name: Parroquia San Ignacio
places.address: Av. Dr. Benito de Miguel 27, Junín
places.sundayHours: Exterior always accessible; Sunday mass schedule varies
places.note: Iglesia matriz on Plaza 25 de Mayo. Exterior photo stop only unless you time a mass.

places.name: Museo Taller Draghi
places.sundayHours: Daily 10:30–13:00 (Google); tourism pages also cite morning windows
places.note: Silverwork museum/workshop facing the plaza — classic SAA stop. Morning-only hours; combine with plaza / Las Lilas, not a late afternoon add-on.

places.name: Parroquia San Isidro Labrador
places.note: Main parish near Plaza San Martín / centro. Easy exterior stop if you already pulled into Chacabuco for the plaza or arco.

places.name: MACA — Museo de Arte Contemporáneo
places.note: Sunday afternoon only. Only fits a return stop through Junín after leaving Lincoln early (~15:30–16:00) — tight for a midnight-back plan.

places.name: Parroquia Inmaculada Concepción
places.note: Gothic façade a block from Plaza Rivadavia. Easy add-on to the central walk.

places.name: Museo De Ayer y Hoy
places.address: Cnel. J.A. Güiraldes 130, San Antonio de Areco
places.note: Perfume-bottle collection museum — weekend-only hours confirmed on sanantoniodeareco.com. Niche but solid if you’re already in SAA.

places.name: Museo Profesor Enrique Urcola
places.sundayHours: Unreliable on Sundays — some listings Sat 18–20 only; call/check Instagram before going
places.note: Distinctive: children's art + Carnaval Artesanal. Worth it if open; skip without confirmation rather than burning the afternoon.

places.name: Parroquia San Andrés Apóstol
places.sundayHours: Exterior always accessible; Google lists Sunday 19:00–20:00 (mass window)
places.note: On Plaza San Martín — the town’s namesake church. Exterior / plaza walk works anytime; interior hours are evening-mass oriented.

places.name: Iglesia Nuestra Sra. del Carmen
places.sundayHours: Exterior always accessible; Sunday mass schedule varies
places.note: On Carmen’s plaza (RN7 town). Nice façade + comic mural nearby. Exterior walk works even if you skip going inside.

places.name: Museo MUBAL
places.note: Sunday opens at 17:00 — only fits if you leave Lincoln later than recommended. Skip for a sane midnight-back plan.

places.name: Teatro Italiano Chacabuco
places.sundayHours: Exterior always accessible; performances by schedule
places.note: Landmark façade near the center. Treat as an exterior look unless there's a Sunday show — not a guaranteed indoor visit.

places.name: MUMPA — Museo de Paleontología
places.address: Inside Parque Natural Laguna de Gómez, Junín
places.note: Only if you already detour to Laguna de Gómez. Same time-tax as the park itself.

places.name: Estación San Andrés de Giles
places.address: Av. J. Morgan 803, San Andrés de Giles
places.note: Historic railway station near the center — classic Giles photo stop mentioned in local guides. Exterior walk only; no need for museum hours.

checkedNote: Researched Jul 9, 2026 via Google Places API (ES), BrightData SERP + page scrapes (sanantoniodeareco.com museums, Lincoln municipal, Giles/Carmen travel guides), and Spanish tourism pages. BD re-check after 407 fix added: Giles church, Chacabuco San Isidro, Lincoln Pepperoni/Simona/Kulto, SAA Draghi + De Ayer y Hoy; Usina Vieja hours set to Sat–Sun 08–20. Skipped as weak for this day trip: MuME Megafauna (late/unclear pin), carnival-only museums, Gouin/Tres Sargentos rural detours, SAA La Recova / Vagues / Villa Lía, Criollo (no solid Places hours). Sunday hours verified where listed; museums can change — confirm before detouring. List filtered to ≥50 Google Maps reviews and sorted by review count descending.
